claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: Mergora DateTimeField with optional native buttons
semantics.naming: A persistent visible label or explicit accessible name identifies every native editor, grid, or collection.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.

interaction.keyboard: Native editor behavior remains authoritative; calendar grids add documented arrows, Home, End, Page Up, and Page Down without intercepting unrelated keys.
interaction.pointer: Every selection path uses native inputs or buttons and preserves cancellation.
interaction.touch: Controls retain preferred coarse-pointer targets and native mobile pickers where available.
interaction.speech: Visible labels and explicit button names remain available for voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: No gesture-only or duplicate proxy action is required.

formBehavior: The underlying native local date-time input owns its local successful value, native constraints, disabled omission, and reset; an enabled wall-time adapter adds custom validity and an explicitly named resolved-instant control.

states.synchronization: Native local value, controlled owner, optional presets, optional descriptive timezone context, and opt-in wall-time resolution under an explicit ambiguity policy remain synchronized.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Strong Ink structure and text state cues accompany color.
preferences.forcedColors: System colors preserve boundaries, selected states, and focus indicators.
preferences.reducedMotion: No essential information or operation depends on motion.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Labels, context, summaries, and localized strings wrap; range layouts collapse without page-level horizontal overflow.

internationalization.imePolicy: Native temporal editors retain browser composition and platform editing behavior.
internationalization.serialization: Successful values use native canonical ASCII date/time/month/year strings; localized text is never submitted in their place.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide persistent visible labels and localize every consumer string.
Validate temporal bounds and canonical values again at the server or trusted boundary.
Choose timezone and DST disambiguation policy explicitly whenever a local value becomes an instant.
Do not treat source-present-unreleased code as published, Stable, or manually assistive-technology verified.
limitations: Generated package, editable source, native registry, Shadcn, and packed-consumer parity have not yet been recorded for this source checkpoint.
Current manual desktop/mobile screen-reader, speech, switch, and real-device records are missing.
The default local date-time value remains unzoned; consumers enabling wallTimeAdapter must supply authoritative, current timezone data and validate the resulting instant at a trusted boundary.
